stories.headline: Kamara Signs Back On at the Saints Ranch, But They Trimmed His Pay
stories.read: Word off the bayou wire, pardner: Alvin Kamara reworked his deal to stay a Saint, takin' his base pay down from near eleven and a half million to six. Now don't go readin' that as a demotion, he's still the lead horse in New Orleans and the man aims to retire in that black and gold. But when a ranch shaves a veteran's wages like that, they're tellin' you plain they mean to ease his load before the miles catch up with him. That makes the spare horse behind him the cheapest insurance policy on the whole Trail, so stake a late claim on that backfield before the saloon wakes up.

stories.headline: Jonathon Brooks Rides Back Healthy, and the Saloon Ain't Pricin' Him Right
stories.read: Big smoke signal outta Carolina: young Jonathon Brooks got the all-clear from the surgeon and looks fixin' to haul the freight for the Panthers this fall. The herd's still grabbin' him deep in the great land grab, spooked by that busted wing from a year back. But a fella with a clear path to the reins and a heavy haul waitin' on him is exactly the vein nobody else has struck. This here's your diamond in the rough, friend, so go pan that creek three rounds before your leaguemates even look up.

stories.headline: Champs Break Camp First: Seahawks Saddle Up With the Cameras Rollin'
stories.read: The scoutin' season's near done, pardner, the great land grab's ridin' up over the ridge. Fresh off hoistin' the big trophy, the Seahawks are the first outfit to break camp when their rookies report July 17, with the Hard Knocks cameras trailin' every step. Here's the nugget worth pannin': speedster Rashid Shaheed and Sam Darnold are hittin' it off, and the new play-caller's fixin' to run more motion. A deep-ball dart at a bargain price while the saloon's barely whisperin' his name, so keep a hungry eye on that claim.
